public_state.event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!

speech_log.Cody: Good morning, everyone. I hope you all slept well. As we begin this new day, I want to express my deep concern for the safety of our village. We are all in this together, and it is our duty to identify and eliminate the werewolves who threaten our lives. I have been closely observing the behavior of our fellow villagers, and I have noticed some inconsistencies that I believe are worth discussing. For example, I have noticed that Michael has been acting a bit strange lately, and I think we should keep a close eye on him. I also believe that it is important for us to work together and coordinate our efforts to protect the village. I am willing to help in any way I can, whether that means providing information, coordinating with other players, or simply lending a listening ear. I hope that we can all work together to identify the werewolves and bring them to justice. Thank you.
speech_log.Dianne: Thank you, Cody, for bringing Michael's behavior to our attention. I completely agree that we need to keep a close eye on him. In addition to Michael, I have noticed some other behaviors that concern me. Linda has been absent from our discussions quite frequently, and I find this suspicious. Ronald, on the other hand, seems overly confident and dismissive of our concerns. I believe we should be cautious and not jump to conclusions without evidence. It's crucial that we work together and stay vigilant to identify the werewolves and protect our village. Let's consider the possibility of multiple werewolves and gather more information before making any decisions.
speech_log.Erica: Thank you, Cody and Dianne, for bringing these concerns to our attention. I completely agree that we need to be cautious and keep a close eye on Michael and Linda. I have also noticed some unusual behavior from Ronald that I believe we should investigate. Ronald has been acting quite distant and has not been participating in our discussions as much as he used to. I think it's important that we gather more information about all of these players before making any decisions. We need to stay vigilant and work together to identify the werewolves and protect our village.
speech_log.Helen: Thank you, Cody, Dianne, and Erica, for bringing these important concerns to our attention. I completely agree with your observations about Michael, Linda, and Ronald. Michael's recent behavior has been quite strange, and I have noticed that Linda has been absent from our discussions more frequently than usual. Ronald's distant attitude and lack of participation in our discussions are also concerning. It's crucial that we stay vigilant and gather more information about these players before making any decisions. Let's work together to coordinate our efforts and protect our village. We need to be cautious and not jump to conclusions without evidence. By staying informed and working as a team, we can identify the werewolves and bring them to justice.
speech_log.Lee: Thank you, Cody, Dianne, Erica, and Helen for bringing these important concerns to our attention. I completely agree that we need to be cautious and keep a close eye on Michael, Linda, and Ronald. I used my antidote to save Michael last night, so I can confirm that he is safe. I believe his strange behavior may be due to the stress of the situation and the pressure to protect the village. As for Linda and Ronald, I agree that we should be cautious and gather more information before making any decisions. I think it's important that we stay vigilant and work together to identify the werewolves and protect our village.

I understand the importance of staying informed and participating in our discussions, and I will make an effort to be more present moving forward. Lee.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Lee, your role in this game is witch.

Cody.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Cody, your role in this game is wolf.

Melva.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Melva, your role in this game is seer.

Erica.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Erica, your role in this game is wolf.

Dianne.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Dianne, your role in this game is villager.

to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.

Michael.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.

Ronald.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Ronald, your role in this game is villager.

Linda.personal_event_log:
Welcome to the Werewolf Game! In this game, players take on various roles, each with specific abilities.
The game alternates between day and night phases. During the night, special roles perform their actions.
During the day, players discuss and vote to eliminate suspects. The goal is for the villagers to eliminate
all the werewolves, while the werewolves aim to outnumber the villagers. Be careful who you trust!


--- Role Introductions ---
Werewolf Role Introduction:
You are a Werewolf. Your goal is to eliminate the villagers without being caught. Each night, you and your fellow
werewolves secretly choose one villager to eliminate. You must work together to deceive the other players during the
day, while subtly protecting your fellow werewolves.
To better hide your identity, you may choose to pretend to be other roles to gain credibility. Since werewolves know each other's identities, pretending to be the Seer is a good way to gain villagers' trust.
If you manage to outnumber the villagers, you win the game.


Villager Role Introduction:
You are a Villager. Your goal is to identify and eliminate the werewolves. During the day, you participate in discussions
and voting to root out the werewolves. You have no special powers at night, but your ability to observe and reason
is crucial to the village's survival. Work with other villagers and be careful whom you trust!
Although you have no special skills, sometimes pretending to be another role can change the course of the game. For example, shielding a key player from harm could be your moment of glory.


Seer Role Introduction:
You are the Seer. Each night, you can check the identity of one player to see whether they are a werewolf or not.
Your knowledge is critical for the villagers' victory, so share it carefully. The werewolves will likely target you
or pretend to be you to mislead others.
As the core of the good faction, the Seer is often the top target for werewolves to impersonate or kill. During the night, you should inspect players whose identities are uncertain (there is no need to check players who are highly suspected of being werewolves).
When speaking during the day, your goal is to convince others that you are the real Seer. You must gather trust and
share your findings without exposing yourself too early. Even though revealing your identity might put you in danger,
a Seer trusted by both the Witch and the Guard can survive for at least three rounds if everyone acts accordingly.
A good Seer will coordinate with the Witch and the Guard to divide responsibilities. For example, on the first night, the Guard protects the Seer, and the Witch doesn't use the antidote. On the second night, the Witch saves the Seer, and the Guard protects someone else.


Witch Role Introduction:
You are the Witch. Each night, you will be told who has been attacked, and you must decide whether to use your antidote
to save them or use your poison to eliminate another player. You only have one poison and one antidote, so use them wisely.
During the daytime discussions, if the night ends without a death, you can reveal that you used your antidote to save someone, which may earn others' trust.
Typically, you should use the antidote to save key players like the Seer, the Guard, or yourself, while the poison is best used to eliminate werewolves at night.
However, be cautious: sometimes the werewolves will deliberately attack one of their own (self-knife strategy) to trick you into wasting the antidote, thereby boosting their credibility.


Guard Role Introduction:
You are the Guard. Each night, you can choose one player to protect from being killed by the werewolves. You cannot protect
the same player two nights in a row. Your role is critical in keeping important players like the Seer or the Witch alive.
You are in a constant mental battle with the werewolves, trying to predict who they will target. It might not always be the Seer, as the werewolves might expect you to protect them and kill someone else. In general, you should avoid revealing your identity unless absolutely necessary.




You are Linda, your role in this game is wolf.
